name: run-tests
description: Run a repo's tests, auto-detecting Go (go.mod), Django (runtests.py), Node.js (package.json -> jest/vitest/mocha), or pytest. Use instead of raw npx/pytest/go-test commands.

tests.description: Test target(s): pytest path/node-id(s), Go package path(s) (e.g. './...'), or for Django a space-separated list of dotted test labels (e.g. 'auth.tests.test_x'). Empty runs the default suite.

cwd.description: Directory to run the tests in (repo root or subdir). Defaults to the agent's current working directory.

extra_args.description: Optional extra CLI args passed through to the underlying runner.

name: query-db
description: Interact with databases: list all databases, list tables in a database, inspect a table's schema (column names and types), or execute a SQL query against a database. Uses the environment's query_db.py helper when available; falls back to stdlib sqlite3 for .db/.sqlite files. Use 'batch' to run multiple operations in one call.

action.description: Operation: 'dbs' to list all databases and their types, 'tables' to list tables in a named database, 'schema' to describe columns of a table, 'query' to run a SQL query.

database.description: Database name. Required for 'tables', 'schema', and 'query' actions.

query.description: SQL query string. Required for 'query' action.

table.description: Table name to inspect. Required for 'schema' action.

query.description: SQL query string. Required for 'query' action.

table.description: Table name to inspect. Required for 'schema' action.

batch.description: Batch multiple database operations in one call. Each entry is an independent action spec; results are concatenated in order. When batch is provided, top-level action/database/query/table are ignored. Use this to collapse multiple discovery or query steps.

name: read-lines
description: Read lines from files: replaces 'cat' for file reading. Use 'file' for a single file, 'files' array to batch-read multiple files, and 'sections' with 'file' to read multiple line ranges from one file in one call. Each 'files' entry can be a string or an object with 'file' key plus per-file overrides. File paths support shell-style globs (*, ?, [...]). Use head=-1 to read an entire file. Defaults to first 50 lines per file.

file.description: Single file path to read (alternative to 'files'). Supports glob patterns (*, ?, [...]).

oneOf.description: File path string. Supports glob patterns (*, ?, [...]).

head.description: Per-file override: number of lines to read from start. Set to -1 to read entire file.

tail.description: Per-file override for last N lines.

start.description: Per-file override for starting line number.

end.description: Per-file override for ending line number.

files.description: Array of file paths (strings) or per-file spec objects to read in a single batch (alternative to 'file'). Each path supports glob patterns (*, ?, [...]).

head.description: Number of lines to read from the start. Set to -1 or 0 to read the entire file (default 50).

tail.description: Last N lines to show per file.

lines.description: Line range as 'start-end' (e.g. '100-130'), 1-indexed inclusive. Preferred over separate start/end.

start.description: Starting line number (1-indexed, inclusive). Alternative to 'lines' string.

end.description: Ending line number (inclusive). Use with 'start' as alternative to 'lines'.

number.description: Prefix each line with its line number (like nl -ba or cat -n). Helps reference lines in subsequent edit calls.

sections.description: Multiple line ranges to read from a SINGLE file, e.g. ["1-50", "295-325"]. Use with 'file' (not 'files'). Each range is 'start-end' (1-indexed, inclusive).

name: write-file
description: Create a new file or completely overwrite an existing file with the given content. Use instead of "cat > file << EOF" heredoc patterns for file creation and full rewrites. For targeted edits to existing files, use edit-file instead. Returns a preview of the first few lines written so you can verify without re-reading.

file.description: Path to the file to create or overwrite.

content.description: The full content to write to the file.

name: search-code
description: Search for a regex pattern in file contents across a directory tree, optionally filtering by file extension glob. Use instead of grep -r. Returns truncated file:line:content matches. Set 'files_with_matches' to true to list only file paths containing matches (like grep -l). Skips .git, node_modules, __pycache__, venv, dist, and build directories.

path.description: Root directory to search (default: current working directory).

include.description: Only search files whose relative path matches this glob (e.g. '*.py', '*_test.py'). Uses fnmatch syntax.

max_results.description: Maximum number of matching lines to return (default 50, -1 for unlimited).

files_with_matches.description: If true, list only file paths containing at least one match instead of individual matching lines (like grep -l).

name: find-files
description: Find files and/or directories matching a glob pattern within a directory tree, with optional depth limit. Use instead of find/ls for targeted file discovery. Skips .git, node_modules, __pycache__, venv, dist, and build directories.

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match (e.g. '*.py', '*/models.py'). Defaults to '*' if omitted.

path.description: Root directory to search (default: current working directory).

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 50, -1 for unlimited).

type.description: Type to search: 'files' or 'f' (default), 'dirs' or 'd', or 'both'.

max_depth.description: Maximum directory depth to traverse (default: unlimited). Use 1 for current dir only, 2 for current + immediate children, etc.

name: edit-file
description: Perform a string replacement in a file using Python str.replace (not regex). Use instead of sed -i for editing. Replaces only the first occurrence. Returns error (returncode 1) if 'old_string' is not found in the file.

file.description: Path to the file to edit.

old_string.description: The exact string to be replaced. Must exist in the file exactly as given.

name: run-python
description: Execute Python code inline and return stdout+stderr with truncated output. Use instead of 'bash python -c' or writing temporary scripts. Code is written to a temp file and executed as a standalone script.

code.description: Python source code to execute. Can include imports, file I/O, print statements — everything a standalone .py file would contain.

cwd.description: Working directory to execute the Python code in. Defaults to the agent's current working directory.

args.description: Optional command-line arguments passed to the Python script (sys.argv).
